body: Indonesia's current account deficit widened to USD 2.15 billion in Q3 2024, an 86% year-over-year increase from USD 1.15 billion, driven by lower goods surplus and higher deficits in services, primary income, and secondary income. However, the deficit improved quarter-to-quarter compared to USD 3.4 billion in Q2 2024, due to higher deficits in services and primary income in the previous quarter.
title: Indonesia's Current Account Deficit Widens 86% Year-on-Year to USD 2.15 Billion in Q3 2024

body: PT Lippo Cikarang Tbk (LPCK) has approved a rights issue of 3 billion new shares, priced at IDR 500 each, to raise capital for working capital and investments in subsidiaries, aiming to boost revenue and profitability. Despite a 3% year-on-year decline in net profit to IDR 71.13 billion in the first half of 2024, the company's marketing sales reached IDR 1.05 trillion, or 74% of its 2024 target, and the rights issue may dilute public investors' stakes by up to 52.82% if they don't participate.
title: PT Lippo Cikarang shareholders approve 3 billion share rights issue to fund business expansion and boost revenue.

body: PT Sunindo Pratama Tbk (SUNI) has secured a credit agreement with PT Bank UOB Indonesia, obtaining an omnibus LC/SKBDN facility worth IDR 25 billion and a construction investment credit facility worth IDR 100 billion for its subsidiary, PT Rainbow Tubulars Manufacture (RTM). The credit facilities will enhance SUNI's financial flexibility and support RTM's completion of its second factory, which is expected to boost OCTG production capacity by 70,000 tons per year and begin operation in Q3 2025.
title: PT Sunindo Pratama's subsidiary signs credit agreement with PT Bank UOB Indonesia to secure IDR 225 billion in facilities for factory construction and working capital.

body: The Indonesian rupiah has depreciated 5.3% against the US dollar over the past two months, dropping to Rp15,945, due to market reaction to Donald Trump's presidential election victory and his policies favoring domestic economic expansion. As a result, global investors are reallocating their portfolios back to the US, leading to capital outflows from emerging markets, including Indonesia, and strengthening the US dollar against currencies such as the Vietnamese dong, Chinese yuan, and Malaysian ringgit.
title: Rupiah Slumps to Lowest Level in Two Months Against US Dollar Amid Global Market Shift.

body: PT Caturkarda Depo Bangunan Tbk (DEPO) targets IDR 3 trillion in revenue by 2025, driven by political stability and expansion of marketing distribution channels, with a projected IDR 2.8 trillion revenue by the end of this year, up from IDR 2.68 trillion in 2022. The company also expects net profit to grow to IDR 88 billion from IDR 86 billion, with plans to open 3 more outlets in 2025, bringing the total to 19 units.
title: PT Caturkarda Depo Bangunan TBK targets IDR 3 trillion revenue in 2025, driven by political stability and expanded marketing channels.

body: PT Hartadinata Abadi Tbk (HRta) is offering a shelf-registration bond worth IDR 900 billion, with two series: Series A with a yield of 6.90% per annum for 3 years and Series B with a yield of 7.10% per annum for 5 years. The proceeds will be used to refinance IDR 480 billion of existing bonds and for working capital, including raw material purchases and store expansions, to support the company's gold jewelry production and refinery operations.
title: PT Hartadinata Abadi Tbk launches IDR 900 billion bond issuance to refinance debt and fund business expansion.

body: PT Adaro Andalan Indonesia Tbk (AADI) plans to raise IDR 4.59 trillion through its initial public offering (IPO) of 778.68 million shares, with 40% of the proceeds allocated to loan its subsidiary, PT Maritim Barito Perkasa, for corporate general purposes and operational activities. The remaining 60% will be used to repay loans to PT Adaro Indonesia and Adaro Energy Indonesia Tbk (ADRO), with the IPO taking place from November 29 to December 3, 2024, and listing on December 5, 2024.
title: Adaro Andalan Indonesia to Allocate IPO Proceeds to Subsidiary Loan and Debt Repayment

body: PT Surya Biru Murni Acetylene Tbk (SBMA) plans to expand its market reach by building gas-filling stations in Sulawesi by 2025, adding to its existing 6 stations in 6 cities in Kalimantan, which is expected to boost its market share and provide more optimum services to users. The expansion is driven by the growing demand for high-quality gas supply in Sulawesi, particularly from the flourishing nickel industry, and is expected to grant added value to shareholders through sustainable growth.
title: PT Surya Biru Murni Acetylene to Expand Market Reach with New Gas-Filling Stations in Sulawesi by 2025.

body: Foreign investors bought a net 769.07 million shares, with top purchases including PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with 871.45 million shares and PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) with 90.69 million shares. Meanwhile, top sells included PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) with -216.18 million shares and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with -78.33 million shares.
title: Foreign investors net buy 769 million shares in Indonesian market, led by digital and e-commerce companies.

body: PT Pelita Sukses Sejati purchased 6.25% shares of PT Sumber Tani Agung Resources Tbk (STAA), acquiring nearly 682 million shares, while Sugiman Halim increased his ownership stake in PT Newport Marine Services Tbk to 10.51% by acquiring 154.51 million shares. Other notable transactions include PT Pangan Sejahtera Investama purchasing 988,500 shares of PT FKS Food Sejahtera Tbk and Inv Management Pte Ltd selling 7.87 million shares of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk.
title: Multiple investors make significant changes to their stakes in various Indonesian companies across different industries.

body: PT Indika Energy Tbk (INDY) expects a disappointing performance in 2024, with revenue down 22.4% year-on-year to $1.8 billion and net profit falling 63.3% to $34.4 million, mainly due to declining coal prices and the absence of contributions from divested coal mine PT Multi Tambang Jaya Utama. The company's coal segment is now solely supported by PT Kideco Jaya Agung, which still contributed 87% of revenue until September 2024, but INDY's strategy to achieve a balanced revenue portion between coal and non-coal segments by 2028 has been delayed from its initial 2025 target.
title: Indika Energy's 2024 Performance to be Affected by Coal Mine Divestment and Declining Coal Prices.

body: PT Pinago Utama Tbk (PINAGO) saw its interim dividend for the 2024 fiscal year decrease by 42.85% to IDR 31.25 billion, compared to IDR 54.68 billion last year, due to a decline in net profit in the third quarter of 2024. The interim dividend of IDR 40 per share will be paid to shareholders, with the company reporting a net profit of IDR 138.38 billion, retained earnings balance of IDR 781.76 billion, and total equity of IDR 893.45 billion in its financial report for September 2024.
title: PT Pinago Utama's Interim Dividend Drops 42.85% to IDR 31.25 Billion in 2024 Fiscal Year.

body: PT Caturkarda Depo Bangunan Tbk (DEPO) is projecting revenue of IDR 2.8 trillion and net profit of IDR 88 billion by the end of next year, driven by the opening of three new outlets in Palembang, Pekanbaru, and Samarinda. The company has allocated IDR 30 billion-IDR 100 billion in capital expenditure for the new outlets, which will increase the total number of Depo outlets to 16 units from 13 units.
title: PT Caturkarda Depo Bangunan to Open Three New Outlets, Targets IDR 2.8 Trillion Revenue by End of Next Year.
